digital-human-2026.title: Digital Human: Your Business Agent Avatar
digital-human-2026.excerpt: From virtual idols to the AI-driven 3.0 era — how digital humans help you expand markets 24/7, reduce content costs, and convert inquiries.

pitch-2026.title: The Time for AI Is Now — Enterprise AI Transformation Guide
pitch-2026.excerpt: A complete guide covering industry trends, architecture, a six-step deployment methodology, and cost-benefit analysis for enterprise decision-makers.

history_milestones.title: The First Virtual Singer
history_milestones.body: Lynn Minmay from the Japanese anime Macross debuted as a virtual idol, pioneering the concept of artificial stars and sparking global imagination around digital humans.

history_milestones.title: Rise of Virtual Idols
history_milestones.body: 2D/3D animated virtual idols led by Hatsune Miku exploded in popularity. Chinese virtual idols like Luo Tianyi and A-SOUL emerged, igniting the ACG scene globally.

history_milestones.title: Brands Enter the Space
history_milestones.body: Hyper-realistic digital humans like Liu Yexi entered brand marketing. Production costs dropped and businesses began piloting digital spokespersons and virtual customer service.

history_milestones.title: Digital Human 3.0
history_milestones.body: Powered by large language models, digital humans evolved from 'players' to 'thinkers.' Real-time interaction, knowledge base integration, and 24/7 streaming — at near-zero marginal cost.

why_items.title: High Costs
why_items.body: Real-person video requires full professional gear — cameras, lighting, sets — plus talent fees. A single quality piece of content can cost thousands of dollars, and a live-streaming team adds substantial monthly payroll.

why_items.title: Efficiency Bottlenecks
why_items.body: Scheduling is difficult, production cycles are long, and round-the-clock content creation is impossible. Running a stable live-streaming room demands large rotating teams, capping output scale.

why_items.title: Uncontrollable Risk
why_items.body: Streamers are inconsistent, influencers can fall from grace overnight, and new talent takes long to develop with high turnover. When a key person leaves, all prior investment can vanish instantly.

capability_items.title: High-Precision Appearance Clone
capability_items.body: Generate a digital human from a single photo at 80% accuracy. Or use AI image generation to create an entirely new virtual identity with no constraints.

capability_items.title: Voice Cloning & Synthesis
capability_items.body: Clone a voice from just a few minutes of audio at 80% accuracy. Or choose a virtual voice with full emotional range — completely digital.

capability_items.title: Auto Motion & Lip Sync
capability_items.body: Fully automatic motion generation driven by text description or motion capture, with automatic lip sync and natural facial expressions.

capability_items.title: Large Model Integration
capability_items.body: Connect to an LLM and the digital human can think and converse — responding to live-stream comments in real time, creating a truly 'soulful' presence.

capability_items.title: Enterprise Knowledge Base
capability_items.body: Link to a corporate knowledge base and the digital human instantly becomes a domain expert, answering professional questions with brand consistency.

capability_items.title: One-Click Outfit & Scene Change
capability_items.body: Freely switch outfits, scenes, and backgrounds. Reuse one asset across multiple contexts to dramatically lower marginal production costs.

capability_items.title: Multilingual Coverage
capability_items.body: Automatic multilingual dubbing with synchronized lip movements. One piece of content reaches global audiences — zero barriers to international business.

capability_items.title: 24/7 Non-Stop
capability_items.body: No fatigue, no sick days, no bad moods. Round-the-clock content output and live streaming, at a unit cost as low as 1/10 that of a real person.

limitation_items.title: Complex Full-Body Motion
limitation_items.body: Highly complex full-body actions like dancing and athletics can still look slightly stiff. Currently best suited for upper-body speaking and dialogue scenes.

limitation_items.title: Hyper-Realistic Production Cost
limitation_items.body: Cinematic-grade hyper-realistic digital humans still carry a high price tag. For mainstream commercial use, semi-realistic or stylized looks offer far better value.

limitation_items.title: Real-World Interaction Compositing
limitation_items.body: Interactions with real physical environments and objects still require post-production compositing — real-time generation is not yet possible.

implementation_steps.title: Build a Long-Term IP Identity
implementation_steps.body: Choose the right appearance (personal photo or AI-generated) and voice (cloned or virtual). This is the foundation of your digital avatar — think long-term from day one.

implementation_steps.title: Design the Virtual Scene
implementation_steps.body: Define backgrounds, effects, and lighting to create a distinct brand visual style. The scene must align tightly with your product tone and target audience.

implementation_steps.title: Write the Script
implementation_steps.body: Drive the digital human with structured slides or plain-text descriptions. The script determines conversion rate — tailor it to different audience segments.

implementation_steps.title: Generate Video / Go Live
implementation_steps.body: One-click render for batch content production. Digital human video is cheap — don't be formulaic; experiment with diverse scenarios.

implementation_steps.title: Edit & Optimize (Optional)
implementation_steps.body: Do secondary editing tailored to each platform's style to improve watch-through rate and engagement. Focus on the first 3-second hook to satisfy algorithm logic.

implementation_steps.title: Distribute · Aggressive Operations
implementation_steps.body: Multi-platform volume is the key. Video is cheap and scenes don't need to be templated — sustained high-frequency output builds algorithm weight and customer mindshare.

implementation_steps.title: Client Development (Active + Passive)
implementation_steps.body: Actively reach target clients and passively capture inbound traffic — bring your ideal audience to your content while simultaneously building a private domain.

implementation_steps.title: Inquiry Conversion
implementation_steps.body: Monetizing traffic is the finish line. Closing scripts, follow-up cadence, and deal flow must all work together. Conversion rate is the ultimate metric.

key_point_items.title: Long-Term Thinking
key_point_items.body: Digital human operations are a long game. Generating content once is not the end — sustained output is what accumulates algorithm weight and builds brand moat.

key_point_items.title: Platform First
key_point_items.body: Content must fit the algorithm logic and user psychology of each platform. Douyin, WeChat Channels, and Bilibili all have different rhythms — adapt accordingly.

key_point_items.title: Conversion Is King
key_point_items.body: Visual polish doesn't matter. What matters is whether you attract precise buyers and drive inquiry conversion. Don't fall into the 'aesthetics trap'.

key_point_items.title: Feel Human
key_point_items.body: The viewer must sense a real person, not a machine. Emotion, tone, rhythm, and pauses — all of these are deliberate design decisions.

key_point_items.title: Scene Alignment
key_point_items.body: Constantly evaluate how outfit, setting, product, and audience fit together. The lower the cognitive friction, the higher the conversion efficiency.

key_point_items.title: Differentiated Targeting
key_point_items.body: Deliver different content to different audiences — vary the scene, selling point, and script. Personalized content reaches the right people with precision.

key_point_items.title: SEO + GEO Synergy
key_point_items.body: Combine traditional SEO with emerging GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) for deeper reach — ensuring your content surfaces in both search and AI-driven answers.

faqs.q: Will platforms penalize or throttle digital human content?
faqs.a: Platforms don't throttle content just because it's AI-generated. What matters is content quality, watch-through rate, and engagement. Authentic, valuable content that fits the platform's rhythm is the key. Some platforms require disclosure of AI generation — check each platform's policy in advance.

faqs.q: Can one digital human serve multiple business scenarios?
faqs.a: Absolutely. By changing outfits, backgrounds, and scripts, the same digital identity can fit multiple product lines and scenarios, dramatically reducing marginal cost. Establish visual guidelines to maintain brand consistency.

faqs.q: Does a more realistic appearance mean higher conversion?
faqs.a: Not necessarily. Hyper-realistic appearances are expensive and can trigger the 'uncanny valley' effect. Cartoon or semi-realistic styles often feel warmer and work better for mass-market contexts. Fit with your audience's aesthetic matters more than photorealism.

faqs.q: Can non-technical people create digital humans?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Today's mainstream tools are user-friendly enough for anyone. Success depends not on technology but on content strategy, operational rhythm, and conversion scripts.

faqs.q: How does digital human live streaming compare to real-person streaming?
faqs.a: Digital human live streaming works especially well for brand accounts, knowledge content, and product explainer formats. For interactive scenarios, connecting an LLM for real-time comment replies dramatically increases authenticity. The best current strategy is 'digital humans for volume + real person for viral hits'.

hero.title: Digital Human: Your Business Agent Avatar
hero.body1: Digital human technology is maturing at unprecedented speed. From generating an appearance from one photo, to cloning a voice, to connecting an LLM for real-time interaction — what once cost millions is now within reach.
hero.body2: It's not just a video tool. It's your 24/7 business agent avatar: never tired, never on leave, never a PR crisis, always opening markets, serving clients, and delivering brand value.
hero.callout1_body: AI empowers digital humans to evolve from 'player' to 'thinker' — real-time interaction, knowledge base access, and 24/7 live streaming.
hero.callout2_body: Compared to real-person production, the marginal cost of an AI digital human approaches zero. Create once, reuse infinitely, distribute everywhere.

pain_points.title: Data Entry & Reconciliation
pain_points.body: Manual data entry and cross-checking is repetitive and error-prone, consuming enormous human effort.

pain_points.title: Scattered Data
pain_points.body: ERP, Excel, and email each hold separate copies. Merging them is tedious, slow, and error-prone.

pain_points.title: Knowledge That Can't Be Retained
pain_points.body: When senior employees leave, processes break. New staff rely on word of mouth, and knowledge never accumulates.

pain_points.title: Month-End Reporting Black Hole
pain_points.body: The busiest periods are spent entirely on mechanical work that could be automated.

pain_points.title: Repetitive Customer Inquiries
pain_points.body: High-frequency, low-complexity questions consume massive customer service resources every day.

pain_points.title: Soaring Training Costs
pain_points.body: More people means more repetition, and efficiency per person never improves.

solution_features.title: PhD-Level Reasoning
solution_features.body: Writing reports, running analysis, and operating software are just baseline capabilities.

solution_features.title: 24/7 On Standby
solution_features.body: No vacation, no fatigue — always responsive, consistently productive.

solution_features.title: Rigorous Review
solution_features.body: Built-in validation and risk logic proactively surfaces potential issues.

solution_features.title: Memory & Retention
solution_features.body: Full history of operations and decisions — always traceable and auditable.

arch_layers.name: Sensory Layer
arch_layers.body: OCR, speech recognition, image recognition, Excel, PPT, browser automation… how AI perceives the external world.

arch_layers.name: Brain Layer
arch_layers.body: Deep reasoning, long-term memory, handling high-complexity problems. Model capability is the foundation.

arch_layers.name: Memory Layer
arch_layers.body: Stores historical data, retrieves context, and enables persistent knowledge.

arch_layers.name: Skills Layer
arch_layers.body: Describe tasks in natural language — like teaching an intern. This is the make-or-break layer for real-world deployment.

steps.title: Identify High-Value Scenarios
steps.body: Start with high-frequency, repetitive work that requires heavy thinking.

steps.title: Tool Design & Integration
steps.body: CLI-ify legacy systems and bridge data gaps — the most overlooked step.

steps.title: Skill Writing & Tuning
steps.body: Describe tasks in natural language, just like writing instructions for an intern.

steps.title: Organizational Alignment
steps.body: Executive sponsorship drives cross-departmental collaboration and removes friction.

steps.title: Go Live
steps.body: Launch at small scale, monitor results, validate ROI.

steps.title: Scale to More Scenarios
steps.body: Once proven, rapidly expand to additional business lines.

security.title: Private Deployment
security.body: Data and models run in the enterprise's own environment. Supports on-premise, private cloud, or hybrid cloud — ideal for regulated industries like finance and healthcare.

security.title: Privacy Protection
security.body: Automatic sensitive data masking, tiered access control, and full operation audit trails.

security.title: Human Checkpoints
security.body: Critical decision nodes require human approval. AI provides recommendations, humans make decisions — accountability always stays with people.

security.title: Accuracy Monitoring
security.body: Built-in evaluation framework continuously monitors accuracy. Real-time efficiency dashboards and regular regression testing prevent quality degradation.

services.title: Bottleneck Identification
services.body: We pinpoint the highest-value entry points to avoid wasting resources on the wrong scenarios.

services.title: Collaboration Tools
services.body: Free collaboration software provided to free teams from cumbersome interfaces.

services.title: Agent Setup
services.body: Build the AI agent runtime environment and assist with token procurement and configuration.

services.title: Skills Training
services.body: Hands-on workflow calibration to prevent pitfalls and accelerate go-live.

services.title: Cost Optimization
services.body: Monitor token usage and continuously optimize inference costs.

services.title: Custom Development
services.body: Deep customization for specialized business scenarios.

services.title: Ongoing Operations
services.body: Continuous issue response, iterative optimization, and IT support.

cases.title: Large Metro System
cases.subtitle: Intelligent Fault Diagnosis
cases.desc: Fault diagnosis across power, warehousing, and dispatch subsystems previously relied on veteran engineers. Now replaced with an AI fault knowledge graph and multi-source data correlation analysis.

cases.title: Enterprise Ticketing System
cases.subtitle: AI Customer Support
cases.desc: Multilingual customer service tickets previously handled manually one by one. Now replaced with AI intent recognition, smart routing, and automated responses.

cases.title: Fully Automated Coding
cases.subtitle: AI Agent Full-Cycle Development
cases.desc: An AI Agent independently completes requirements analysis, coding, testing, and deployment — transitioning from human-dependent coding to fully autonomous execution.

faqs.a: We support private deployment — data never leaves your enterprise boundary. Full end-to-end encryption with complete audit trails.

It depends on your goals. If the business is growing, AI supports more output. If the goal is efficiency, repetitive roles will be reduced. Junior, high-repetition positions will see their value diluted.

faqs.q: What if the investment doesn't work out?
faqs.a: We use a 'small-cut validation' strategy: validate one scenario with an MVP first, see results in 2–4 weeks, then scale — minimizing risk.

faqs.q: What if we don't have a tech team?
faqs.a: You don't need one. Skills are written in natural language and we provide end-to-end support.

faqs.q: What if AI output is inaccurate?
faqs.a: Human checkpoints are set at critical decision nodes — AI recommends, humans decide. Built-in accuracy monitoring runs continuously.

faqs.q: Can it integrate with our existing systems?
faqs.a: Yes. We CLI-ify legacy systems through tool design, with experience integrating many common enterprise platforms.

industry.title: 2026: AI Hits Full Scale
industry.body1: AI adoption in the tech industry is universal. Aggressive enterprises are already achieving 2–3x efficiency gains. This transformation is spreading from tech into finance, law, accounting, and every knowledge-intensive sector.
industry.body2: Businesses are broadly launching AI deployments. The window is narrowing — the later you start, the higher the catch-up cost. The question is no longer 'whether to do it,' but 'how to do it right.'
industry.callout1_body: The later you start, the higher the catch-up cost. Early movers have already built data and experience moats.
industry.callout2_body: The question has shifted from 'should we deploy AI?' to 'how do we deploy it correctly?'

research.title: The Data Speaks
research.sub: Latest research from McKinsey, Gartner, and Anthropic
research.anthropic_body: Analysis of the gap between theoretical AI capability and actual adoption by occupation. AI capability already exceeds real-world usage across many roles — meaning most enterprises are leaving productivity on the table.

solution.title: AI Digital Workers: 24/7, Tireless, PhD-Level Reasoning
solution.sub: An AI digital worker is not just a tool — it's a digital colleague with perception, reasoning, memory, and execution.

architecture.title: The Four Layers of an AI Agent
architecture.sub: A complete AI Agent has four layers. The most critical is not the brain (model) — it's the skills layer.
architecture.wf_body: Highly predictable. Ideal for well-bounded, fixed-step tasks: content generation, classification routing, quality checks. Decision-making driven by code.
architecture.agent_body: Highly flexible. Ideal for complex, open-ended, multi-step reasoning tasks: research, anomaly handling, open-ended problems. Decision-making driven by the model.
